data.title: THE FOUR ROUTES.
blocks: Chat
Cowork
Claude Code
Human-only

S04.meta: // SLIDE 04 — WHAT MAKES A TASK COWORK-SHAPE

data.title: WHAT MAKES A TASK COWORK-SHAPED.
blocks: · Multiple source files that all need to be read
· An output that must be saved rather than just displayed
· More than one or two steps in sequence
· A result you can define in advance and verify afterward
· Source materials in a bounded, accessible folder

data.title: THE ROUTING MATRIX.
blocks: Need ideas, draft text, or critique — Chat
Need files assembled into an artifact — Cowork
Need code changes, tests, or a diff — Claude Code
Need final judgment on a person's situation — Human-only

S06.meta: // SLIDE 06 — THE IRONIES OF ROUTING WRONG

data.title: THE IRONIES OF ROUTING WRONG.
blocks: Bainbridge's classic 1983 paper "Ironies of Automation" identified a trap that applies directly to Cowork routing: the m
The Ironies of Routing Wrong — Routing a task to chat when it should be Cowork wastes your time on manual assembly. Routing a task to Cowork when it sh

S07.meta: // SLIDE 07 — THINKING THROUGH THE FOUR CASE

data.title: THINKING THROUGH THE FOUR CASES.
blocks: Memo rewrite:
Operations packet:

S08.meta: // SLIDE 08 — WHEN CHAT IS ENOUGH — AND THAT

data.title: WHEN CHAT IS ENOUGH — AND THAT IS GOOD
blocks: · Drafting short documents from materials you paste directly
· Brainstorming, critiquing, and explaining
· Summarizing a single document you can share
· Answering questions with reasoning or analysis
· Revising text with feedback
data.section: WHEN CHAT IS ENOUGH — AND THAT

data.title: HUMAN-ONLY IS NOT FAILURE.
blocks: Decisions with real consequences for real people personnel, accommodations, benefits, legal status
Regulated information health records, student records, financial filings, legal documents
Irreversible actions deleting files permanently, sending external communications, making commitments on behalf of others
Ethically ambiguous judgment situations where values, context, and relationships matter more than pattern matching

S10.meta: // SLIDE 10 — THE HUMAN GATE IN THIS CHAPTER

data.title: THE HUMAN GATE IN THIS CHAPTER.
blocks: · Is this task mainly conversation, or does it require files and a saved artifact?
· Does it require codebase action?
· Does it involve sensitive, regulated, or irreversible consequences?
Is there a clear verification path could I check whether the output is right?
data.section: THE HUMAN GATE IN THIS CHAPTER

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: "Cowork is always better than chat."
"The most powerful surface is the right surface."
"A repetitive task is automatically safe to delegate."
"Code work is just another document task."
"Human-only is a failure to use AI."

data.title: THE CORE CLAIM.
blocks: "Cowork is always better than chat." Cowork has setup cost — workspace, brief, plan, review. For a simple task, that cos
"The most powerful surface is the right surface." Routing by power rather than fit is how work gets slower, not faster.
